witnesses.description: A one-sided exact binomial test with n = 4, k = 3, p0 = 1/2.
A fixed 2x2 table with exact hypergeometric point and left-tail probabilities.

checks.claim: Under Binomial(4, 1/2), the probability of at least 3 successes is 5/16.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes C(4,3)/16 + C(4,4)/16 exactly.

checks.claim: The listed 2x2 table has exact hypergeometric point probability 8/35 under fixed margins.
The exact one-sided Fisher left-tail p-value for the listed table is 17/70.
checks.notes: The validator sums the fixed-margin hypergeometric probabilities for top-left counts 0 and 1.

checks.claim: The same Binomial(4, 1/2) right-tail p-value cannot be 1/4.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the exact tail probability as 5/16, so the claimed value 1/4 is rejected.

checks.claim: The Binomial(4, 1/2) right-tail numerator for X >= 3 can be 4.

data.certificate: produce_diophantine_evidence emits Evidence::UnsatDiophantine checked by Evidence::check for the integer count contradiction tail_count = 4 + 1 and tail_count = 4

checks.notes: The SMT-LIB artifact encodes the rejected p-value numerator as an integer count contradiction. Axeyum emits a Diophantine certificate for the inconsistent equalities.
